channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Google to use UK and EU user IP addresses for ad personalization
item.description: From August 3, 2026, Google will use IP addresses from UK, EEA and Switzerland users for ad measurement and personalization. It lands as the ICO weighs new consent rules, and years after Google itself...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: INC Ransomware Thrives by Mastering the Basics
item.description: And one of those basics is focusing on sectors where a ransomware disruption creates immediate pressure to pay up, like with healthcare....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments
item.description:
An unknown threat actor has been observed leveraging paid or promoted posts on legitimate news websites to drum up buzz for their warez, according to new findings from Check Point Research.
The threa...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
item.description:
Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet.
The vulnerability has now been assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-50656 (...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Junior Hacker Used Tailscale and OpenSSH to Keep Access After His C2 Went Offline
item.description:
A French-speaking attacker broke into a small French automotive business, planted a keylogger, and stole banking and email credentials.
Ordinary stuff, until one move near the end.
Before his comman...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
item.description: A newly discovered data leak dubbed "FortiBleed" has exposed what appears to be a collection of Fortinet and FortiGate VPN credentials for 73,932 firewall URLs at organizations worldwide. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Adversarial Exposure Validation Turns Security Visibility into Confident Prioritization
item.description:
For security teams, the findings never stop, but confidence in knowing which ones matter is becoming harder to maintain.
The problem is no longer visibility. It's validation. Security teams must deci...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
item.description: Attackers are actively targeting various sectors across nearly 200 countries and already have compiled a list of working credentials for tens of thousands of compromised devices....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Why Account Takeovers Are Rising and How to Stop Them
item.description: Account takeovers are rising as attackers bypass traditional defenses through phishing, session hijacking, and MFA fatigue. Specops Software explores how device trust and continuous verification help ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI API Keys as Chrome Extensions Capture Chatbot Chats
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a "coordinated malware campaign" on the JetBrains Marketplace that has published no less than 15 malicious plugins capable of exfiltrating artificial intelligenc...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: India's Telegram ban hit the UAE too. Here's how to get around it
item.description: India has banned Telegram until June 22 after the app was used to circulate leaked exam papers. CEO Pavel Durov accuses telecom Reliance of BGP hijacking that disrupted the app as far away as the UAE....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft confirms Office apps launch issues after June updates
item.description: Microsoft is investigating a new issue preventing third-party applications from launching Microsoft Office applications or opening documents on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Top 10 Attack Surface Exposures in 2026
item.description: Breaches don't always start with a zero-day. An exposed admin panel can get brute-forced, or credentials reused from a previous attack. But when a vulnerability does drop — like MongoBleed earlier thi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch max severity Joomla plugin flaw by Friday
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered federal agencies to patch a maximum-severity flaw in the Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) plugin that is being ac...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft working on Defender patch for RoguePlanet zero-day
item.description: Microsoft confirmed that it's working on a security patch for a Defender zero-day vulnerability named "RoguePlanet," disclosed one week ago. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UK Social Media Ban for Minors Has Privacy Experts Worried
item.description: The UK will ban adolescents under 16 years old from user-to-user social media platforms, despite age verification issues and privacy concerns....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 144 Mastra npm Packages Compromised via Hijacked Contributor Account
item.description: As many as 144 npm packages associated with the Mastra namespace ("@mastra/*"), a popular open-source JavaScript and TypeScript framework for building artificial intelligence (AI) applications, have b...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Kodak confirms data breach claimed by ShinyHunters extortion gang
item.description: Kodak has confirmed that it's working with external cybersecurity experts to investigate a security breach after hackers gained access to some of the company's data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Joomla JCE Flaw Allowing PHP Code Execution
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Fileless Phantom Stealer Targets Browser Credentials
item.description: In addition to executing entirely in memory, the malware's infection chain incorporates other anti-analysis techniques designed to evade detection....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Security Community Slams US Ban on Exporting Mythos, Fable
item.description: An open letter signed by dozens of security experts asked the government to reverse export restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Malicious JetBrains Marketplace plugins steal AI API keys from developers
item.description: At least 15 malicious plugins found on the JetBrains Marketplace were designed to steal AI API keys from developers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SprySOCKS Windows Variant Abuses Kernel Drivers to Evade Detection
item.description: FishMonger, a China-nexus threat group, has deployed an undocumented version of the Linux backdoor against government targets in Honduras, Taiwan, Thailand, and Pakistan....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Rokarolla Android malware targets 217 banking, crypto apps
item.description: A new Android banking trojan named Rokarolla is targeting 217 banking and cryptocurrency applications using an extensive set of 137 commands. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google Vertex AI SDK Flaw Let Attackers Hijack Model Uploads via Bucket Squatting
item.description: A flaw in the Google Cloud Vertex AI SDK for Python let an attacker with no access to a victim's project hijack the victim's machine learning model upload and run code inside Google's serving infrastr...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Steam Workshop abused to spread malware via Wallpaper Engine app
item.description: Threat actors are abusing Steam Workshop, Valve's community hub for downloading game-related content, to push various malware hidden in wallpaper packages. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ClickFix Campaigns Expand Malware Delivery With New Loaders and Fake Update Lures
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged multiple ClickFix campaigns that deliver three malware loaders called BabaDeda Loader, Lorem Ipsum Loader, and Potemkin, per independent reports from Morphisec, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Rokarolla Android Trojan Levels Up to Full Device Control, Persistence
item.description: The emerging malware, spread via fake TikTok and Chrome downloads, has evolved by combining banking fraud with extensive device surveillance and remote control....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 'Lorem Ipsum' Malware Pivots to ClickFix Delivery
item.description: New analysis shows the campaign, which uses compromised WordPress sites, may be linked to the ransomware and data extortion group Vice Society....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: UK to require ID or face scan before you can make social media accounts
item.description: Opening a new social media account in the UK will soon mean proving you're over 16 with an ID upload or a facial age scan, under a government ban on under-16s taking effect in spring 2027. Security ex...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
